thought_beat_text: The fingerprint in the clay, the foam-core edge—he sees them now with a clarity that feels like betrayal, and the weight of that seeing presses against his throat because speaking it would mean admitting the whole stage is permission, not truth. He wants to reverse it, to unknow, but the reversal itself would require saying the thing that cannot be said.
residue_summary: The seams are visible and irrevocable; the only path forward runs through the admission he cannot make without collapsing the world he shares with others.
image_hint: light catching the edge of cardboard, a hand hovering just short of touching the exposed foam
audio_hint: held breath under stage light hum, a single note sustained past comfort
